Empires & Puzzles Guan Yu Review: The Valiant General Strategy Guide

Guan Yu was once the undisputed king of green damage. Years later, does his Green Dragon Crescent Blade still cut deep enough? We analyze his unique minion-bypassing mechanics and whether this Valiant General deserves a spot on your roster today.
Guan Yu Review: The God of War vs. The Minion Meta
Guan Yu: The Valiant General Who Ignores Your Minions
Guan Yu is a legendary Nature (Green) hero from the

family. Released back in the spring of 2022, he quickly established himself as a premier damage dealer, famous for simply ignoring the existence of meat shields. He represents the "Valiant General" archetype—hitting hard, debuffing defense, and generally not caring about the rules of engagement regarding minions. While he has since received a costume (reviewed separately), this guide focuses on his
form, which remains a Paladin with a very specific set of skills.

Guan Yu is depicted in his classic "God of War" attire: flowing green robes, a magnificent beard that probably has its own grooming budget, and his legendary Green Dragon Crescent Blade. The art style is vibrant and heroic, fitting for a figure of his historical and mythological stature. Compared to his costume, which goes full "Inferno" mode with blue and gold accents, the base version feels more traditional and grounded—if you can call a man wielding a 100-pound glaive "grounded."
Let's be brutally honest: by 2026 standards, base Guan Yu's stats are showing their age.
Once considered high, it now pales in comparison to modern hitters pushing 1300+ base attack.
These are dangerously low for a slow-paced meta. Without Limit Breaks (LB1/LB2) and emblems, he is squishy.
To make him viable in diamond raids today, he
full investment (LB2). However, his high damage percentage helps mitigate the lower attack stat somewhat.
Special Skill: Green Dragon Crescent Blade
Guan Yu’s special is a masterclass in "Anti-Minion" warfare.
This is still a respectable multiplier. Hitting three targets for nearly 400% is significant, especially since he doesn't waste damage on minions.

This is his crown jewel. While other heroes struggle to chew through 1000+ HP of Mega Minions or layers of elf minions, Guan Yu hits the hero directly. This makes him an assassin who can finish off weakened targets hiding behind a wall of summoned units.

The scaling defense down (-25% to -70%) is technically uncapped in potential but practically capped by the number of minions. In a minion-heavy war (like Undead Horde), this debuff can become devastatingly strong (-70% is game-ending). However, against teams with zero minions, a flat -25% is merely "okay."
The fact that the defense down cannot be cleansed is huge. It forces the enemy to sit with a broken defense for 3 turns, leaving them vulnerable to a follow-up sniper.
Can gain mana when minions are summoned for him (rare without a minion summoner ally).
Needs a Level 23 Mana Troop (or equivalent) to shave a tile off, making him effectively 9 tiles.
Compared to a modern "Hit All" doing 400% to 5 (2000% total), he lags behind in total output, but his concentrated damage on 3 targets is often better for securing kills.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Kingdom family bonus is a bit odd for Guan Yu specifically. He gains Mana and Healing when a minion is summoned

. Since he doesn't summon minions himself, this bonus does absolutely nothing unless you pair him with a minion summoner (like Liu Bei, Hulda, or Miriam & Midnight). If you do, he becomes significantly faster and tankier.
Base Guan Yu does not have the "Unstoppable Minions" passive found on his costume.
He relies solely on the Family Bonus.

is excellent for him. It adds raw damage to his special skill for the first 6 turns. Since he is an Average speed hero, you want his first hit to count. This helps bridge the gap between his older stats and modern HP pools.
Upon release, Guan Yu was a "Must Have." He broke the minion meta defined by Bera and Freya. He was the green hammer everyone feared.
Today, he is no longer the top dog. Heroes like
, or modern Construct/Astral heroes output more raw damage or faster utility.

. He is no longer a general-purpose defense hero. However, on offense, specifically against Minion-heavy teams (Waterpipe, Hulda, Queen of Hearts), his "Bypass" mechanic is timeless. He will always have a job as a "Can Opener" for minion teams.
The Costume is a massive upgrade. We are talking +300-400 points in stats. If you have the costume, the stats alone are a reason to switch.
(Bleed). Paladin is arguably better for keeping him alive, which is his main struggle.
Costume wins on damage and debuff potency.
Costume gets "Unstoppable Minions," Base gets nothing.

is superior in almost every way due to the stats and higher debuff floor. However, if you desperately need the Paladin defense boost to survive a sniper hit, the Base form has a tiny niche. But really... use the Costume.
Guan Yu is an attacker. His job is to kill things.
Maximize his Paladin class talent (Defense boost on damage). Keeps him alive to fire twice.
His damage might fail to secure a kill against LB2 healers.
Maximizes the 390% hit. Necessary to pierce modern defense stats.

Evelyn/Almur (EDD) -> Guan Yu (DD) -> Fast Sniper (e.g., Francine/Lianna)
Elemental Defense Down followed by Guan Yu's uncleansable Defense Down sets up a devastating combo. Guan Yu hits 3, softening them up for the sniper to finish one.
Grimble eats minions to give mana -> Guan Yu bypasses remaining minions to debuff defense -> Skadi kills the weakened minions to stack her frost.

If you pulled him early, rejoice! He is a solid A-tier hero for map stages and early raiding. Focus on keeping him alive with healers. Don't worry about the minion scaling mechanics too much early on; a 390% hit is just strong.

If you don't have the costume, Base Guan Yu is a situational tool. He lives on your bench until you see a Hulda or Waterpipe tank in War. He is not worth Alpha Aethers (LB2) unless you have the costume or lack other green options.
"He’s got the highest scaling defense down in the game. I really want this hero." - A hopeful player in 2022.
"I find it very difficult to achieve the high end of his defense debuff." - Many players realized that getting the full -70% is a rare dream scenario.
"Is he better than Quenell?" This was the big debate of 2022. The consensus was Quenell for raw speed/damage, Guan Yu for utility.

Base Guan Yu is an aging general. He still has the skills to pay the bills—specifically the "Ignore Minions" bill—but his armor is rusty (low stats). He is a tactical nuke against specific teams rather than a universal soldier. If you have him, cherish him for those minion wars. If you have his costume, put this version in the closet and never look back.
A niche counter-hero who still does his one job very well.
Is Guan Yu still worth ascending in 2026? A detailed review of the War of Three Kingdoms hero, his bypass minion mechanics, and how he holds up in the current meta.
